name: Camp Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 gallon Whole tomatoes plus 2 regular cans tomatoes
1 gallon Whole corn plus 2 regular cans corn
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the beef and pork roast until tender.
2. Remove excess fat from the chickens and boil them, leaving the skin on.
3. While the meat is boiling, cut the potatoes into 1/2 - 3/4-inch squares.
4. Cut the tomatoes into slices (or dice as desired).
5. Cut the onions into medium-sized squares.
6. Place the potatoes, onions, and tomatoes with most of the tomato juice in a large pot to cook.
7. Do not add corn at this stage.
8. Tear the meat apart and shred it very fine. Remove all fat, bones, and skin.
9. Mix the shredded meat together.
10. When the potatoes, tomatoes, and onions are done cooking, alternate layers of stew and meat in a large container to mix.
11. Add ketchup and cocktail sauce to the mixture.
12. Mix or stir well. If additional juice is needed, use broth from the meats.
13. Place the contents back into the pot, add corn, and simmer until the corn is cooked through.
14. Be cautious that the stew may stick and burn easily at this stage.
15. When the corn is done, the camp stew is ready to serve. (Freezes well)
